claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit normally stops when a child turns 20, unless they remain in approved education or training, but even then it only continues until they turn 20.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me make my determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Eligibility Assessment for Alex:**

I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Reason:** Alex is 21 years old, which exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit. According to the rules:
- Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16
- For children aged 16 or over, Child Benefit can continue until they turn 20 **only if** they remain in approved education or training

Since Alex is 21 years old, he is beyond the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of his education or training status.

If you have any other questions or if there are other circumstances you'd like to discuss, please let me know.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. If your child is 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, even if he were in approved education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. As Alex is 21, he exceeds the age limit and the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. If your child is 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, even if he were in approved education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. As Alex is 21, he exceeds the age limit and the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
